questions.question: You are seeing a 'Policy Enforced' error when trying to use Notion Mail. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: You are using a private relay email address.
options.explanation: Private relay emails are mentioned as a separate issue, but a Policy Enforced error specifically refers to organizational app authorization.

options.text: Your organization does not authorize apps with the permissions requested by Notion Mail.
options.explanation: According to the article, a Policy Enforced error indicates that your organization's settings prevent apps from accessing the necessary permissions.

options.text: The Advanced Protection Program on your Google account is turned on.
options.explanation: The Advanced Protection Program is mentioned as a separate requirement that must be turned off, not the cause of the policy error.

options.text: Notion Mail has been turned off for your workspace.
options.explanation: Notion Mail being turned off is a different scenario, usually requiring an organization owner to re-enable it.

questions.question: If Notion Mail has been turned off for your workspace, who is responsible for turning it back on?

options.text: You (if you have admin privileges)
options.explanation: The article specifies that an 'organization owner' must turn the feature on, implying standard users cannot enable it.

options.text: Notion Support
options.explanation: The article directs users to ask the organization owner; it does not suggest contacting support for this specific issue.

options.text: Any user in the workspace
options.explanation: Notion Mail is a workspace-wide setting, so standard users cannot toggle it on or off.

options.text: An organization owner
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that an organization owner can turn Notion Mail on for the organization.

questions.question: Which Google security feature must be disabled to use Notion Mail?

options.text: 2-Step Verification
options.explanation: Two-factor authentication is not mentioned as a requirement to disable; only the Advanced Protection Program is.

options.text: Google's Advanced Protection Program
options.explanation: The article states that if you are using Google's Advanced Protection Program, you must turn it off to use Notion Mail.

options.text: Private Relay
options.explanation: Private Relay is an email service provider feature, not a Google account security setting that needs to be disabled for this context.

options.text: Login with Google
options.explanation: The article does not suggest disabling the ability to sign in with Google, only the Advanced Protection Program.

questions.question: What happens if you use a private relay email to access Notion Mail?

options.text: You can access it but cannot send emails.
options.explanation: The article states you 'won't be able to access' Notion Mail at all, not just that sending is restricted.

options.text: You can access it but must use a different login method.
options.explanation: The article states that private relay emails are incompatible with the service.

options.text: You cannot access Notion Mail.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'If you're using a private relay email, you won't be able to access Notion Mail.'

options.text: You must turn off 2-Step Verification first.
options.explanation: While 2-Step Verification is good practice, the specific restriction regarding private relay is about the email provider itself.

questions.question: Once an organization owner turns on Notion Mail for your organization, how long does it typically take for you to sign up?

options.text: Immediately
options.explanation: The article notes that it may take time for the feature to be fully available to the user.

options.text: 1 hour
options.explanation: The article specifies a longer wait time.

options.text: Around 24 hours
options.explanation: The article states: 'Once this is done, it may take around 24 hours for you to be able to sign up.'

options.text: 1 week
options.explanation: The article does not mention a week-long wait time.

questions.question: If the email you use for Notion does not match the email you used to sign up for Notion Mail, what is the recommended solution?

options.text: Change your Notion password.
options.explanation: Password changes are unrelated to the email address mismatch described in the troubleshooting steps.

options.text: Delete your Notion account and create a new one.
options.explanation: The article suggests a simpler method of adding another account rather than deleting the primary one.

options.text: Add another account via the dropdown next to your name in Notion Mail.
options.explanation: The article instructs users to 'add another account by opening the dropdown next to your name in Notion Mail' to resolve the mismatch.

options.text: Contact support to merge the accounts.
options.explanation: The article provides a direct UI action (adding an account) rather than suggesting a support ticket.

questions.question: Why is it important that the email used in Notion matches the email used for Notion Mail?

options.text: It is required to use the same password for both services.
options.explanation: The article does not mention password synchronization as a requirement for login.

options.text: Notion Mail requires a verified email to function correctly.
options.explanation: The article explains that if emails don't match, you cannot access the service, implying a verification or matching requirement.

options.text: It allows for faster sync between the two apps.
options.explanation: While emails matching helps with account management, the article frames the mismatch as a barrier to accessing the service entirely.

options.text: It prevents you from being locked out of your Notion workspace.
options.explanation: The issue is specific to the Notion Mail feature, not the general Notion workspace login.
